\subparagraph{RUNS REPOSITORY \textendash{} Handles insertion of run data and derived metrics}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.repositories.runs:runs-repository-handles-insertion-of-run-data-and-derived-metrics}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains all logic for inserting runs into the database, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Extracting data from harness output
\sphinxhyphen{} Computing derived metrics (energy\_per\_instruction, thermal\_delta, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} Computing cryptographic run state hash
\sphinxhyphen{} Inserting the main run record

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Separates run insertion logic from other database operations

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Makes the 80+ column insert manageable

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralizes derived metric calculations

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Part of splitting the god object manager.py

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains logic for computing and storing orchestration tax summaries.
Pairs linear and agentic runs and calculates tax = agentic \sphinxhyphen{} linear.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Separates tax logic from other database operations

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tax is a research concept that may evolve

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Part of splitting the god object manager.py

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\paragraph{core.database.base module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.base:module-core.database.base}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.base:core-database-base-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.base::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.base@\spxentry{core.database.base}}\index{core.database.base@\spxentry{core.database.base}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DATABASE INTERFACE \textendash{} Abstract Base Class for All Database Adapters}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.base:database-interface-abstract-base-class-for-all-database-adapters}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines a common interface that all database adapters (SQLite, PostgreSQL)
must implement. This allows switching databases by simply changing the
config file, without modifying any application code.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To support future migration from SQLite to PostgreSQL

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To keep database code modular and testable

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To enforce consistent method signatures across adapters

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To implement the adapter pattern for database flexibility

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{UNITS AND ASSUMPTIONS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All time values are in nanoseconds

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All energy values are in microjoules

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods raise DatabaseError on failures

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Context manager support for transactions

\end{itemize}

\end{description}


\subparagraph{AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.base:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{DatabaseError@\spxentry{DatabaseError}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\index{DatabaseInterface (class in core.database.base)@\spxentry{DatabaseInterface}\spxextra{class in core.database.base}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.database.base:core.database.base.DatabaseInterface}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.database.base.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DatabaseInterface}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ABC}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Abstract base class for all database adapters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Any new database adapter (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL) must implement
all methods defined here. This ensures the rest of the application
can interact with any database seamlessly.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The interface is divided into sections:
1. Connection management
2. Transaction handling
3. Table creation
4. Core data insertion
5. Query methods
6. Context manager support

{}
{{ $\rightarrow$ None}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create all necessary tables if they don’t exist.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For SQLite, this runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements.
For PostgreSQL, this may use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or migrations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Tables to create:
\sphinxhyphen{} experiments
\sphinxhyphen{} hardware\_config
\sphinxhyphen{} idle\_baselines
\sphinxhyphen{} runs
\sphinxhyphen{} orchestration\_events
\sphinxhyphen{} orchestration\_tax\_summary
\sphinxhyphen{} energy\_samples
\sphinxhyphen{} cpu\_samples
\sphinxhyphen{} interrupt\_samples

\sphinxAtStartPar
Also creates indexes and views (ml\_features).

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Create tax summary entries for all agentic runs in an experiment.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method:
1. Finds all runs in the experiment
2. Pairs linear and agentic runs by run\_number
3. Computes tax = agentic\_dynamic\_uj \sphinxhyphen{} linear\_dynamic\_uj
4. Stores results in orchestration\_tax\_summary table

\paragraph{core.database.factory module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:module-core.database.factory}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:core-database-factory-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.factory@\spxentry{core.database.factory}}\index{core.database.factory@\spxentry{core.database.factory}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DATABASE FACTORY \textendash{} Creates appropriate database adapter based on configuration}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:database-factory-creates-appropriate-database-adapter-based-on-configuration}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Factory pattern implementation that selects and creates the correct
database adapter (SQLite, PostgreSQL, etc.) based on the ‘engine’
setting in the configuration file.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralizes adapter selection logic

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Makes it easy to add new database types

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Decouples adapter creation from usage

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Follows the factory design pattern

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{USAGE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
config = config\_loader.get\_db\_config()
db = DatabaseFactory.create(config)
db.connect()
db.create\_tables()

\end{description}


\subparagraph{AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{DatabaseFactory (class in core.database.factory)@\spxentry{DatabaseFactory}\spxextra{class in core.database.factory}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:core.database.factory.DatabaseFactory}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.database.factory.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DatabaseFactory}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Factory class for creating database adapters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This factory examines the ‘engine’ key in the configuration
and instantiates the appropriate adapter class.
\index{create() (core.database.factory.DatabaseFactory class method)@\spxentry{create()}\spxextra{core.database.factory.DatabaseFactory class method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.database.factory:core.database.factory.DatabaseFactory.create}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{classmethod}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{create}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ {\hyperref[\detokenize{core.database.base:core.database.base.DatabaseInterface}]{\sphinxcrossref{DatabaseInterface}}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create and return an appropriate database adapter.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{config}} \textendash{} Database configuration dictionary containing:
\sphinxhyphen{} engine: str \sphinxhyphen{} ‘sqlite’ or ‘postgresql’
\sphinxhyphen{} engine\sphinxhyphen{}specific config under the engine key
\sphinxhyphen{} Common settings at top level

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An instance of a class implementing DatabaseInterface

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{core.database.base:core.database.base.DatabaseError}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{DatabaseError}}}}} \textendash{} If engine is not supported or config is invalid

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Example}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{config = \{}
\sphinxAtStartPar
‘engine’: ‘sqlite’,
‘sqlite’: \{
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
‘path’: ‘data/experiments.db’,
‘journal\_mode’: ‘WAL’
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\}
db = DatabaseFactory.create(config)

\end{fulllineitems}

\paragraph{core.database.manager module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager:module-core.database.manager}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager:core-database-manager-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.manager@\spxentry{core.database.manager}}\index{core.database.manager@\spxentry{core.database.manager}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DATABASE MANAGER \textendash{} Lightweight orchestrator for database operations}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager:database-manager-lightweight-orchestrator-for-database-operations}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a unified interface to the database layer by coordinating:
\sphinxhyphen{} Database adapter (SQLite/PostgreSQL)
\sphinxhyphen{} Repositories for different data domains
\sphinxhyphen{} Transaction management
\sphinxhyphen{} Connection lifecycle

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Single entry point for all database operations

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hides complexity of repositories from callers

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Manages adapter lifecycle

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Follows facade pattern

\end{itemize}

\end{description}


\subparagraph{AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{DatabaseManager (class in core.database.manager)@\spxentry{DatabaseManager}\spxextra{class in core.database.manager}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager:core.database.manager.DatabaseManager}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.database.manager.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DatabaseManager}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Main interface to the A‑LEMS database.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class orchestrates all database operations by delegating to:
\sphinxhyphen{} A database adapter (SQLite/PostgreSQL)
\sphinxhyphen{} Specialized repositories for different data domains
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
config = config\_loader.get\_db\_config()
db = DatabaseManager(config)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Insert data
exp\_id = db.insert\_experiment(experiment\_data)
run\_id = db.insert\_run(exp\_id, hw\_id, run\_data)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Query data
runs = db.get\_runs\_by\_experiment(exp\_id)
ml\_data = db.get\_ml\_data()

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Transaction management
with db.transaction():
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
db.insert\_hardware(hw\_data)
db.insert\_baseline(baseline\_data)
\end{quote}

\end{description}

\paragraph{core.database.manager\_oldversion module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager_oldversion:module-core.database.manager_oldversion}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager_oldversion:core-database-manager-oldversion-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager_oldversion::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.manager\_oldversion@\spxentry{core.database.manager\_oldversion}}\index{core.database.manager\_oldversion@\spxentry{core.database.manager\_oldversion}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DATABASE MANAGER \textendash{} Module 4: Persistent Storage for A‑LEMS Experiments}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.manager_oldversion:database-manager-module-4-persistent-storage-for-alems-experiments}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a complete SQLite database interface for storing
all experiment data generated by the A‑LEMS system. It implements the
final 10‑table schema designed for research‑grade analysis, including
runs, orchestration events, high‑frequency samples, tax summaries,
and a flattened ML view.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY IT EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To enable long‑term storage and reproducibility of all measurements.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To support GUI queries (fast, indexed) for interactive exploration.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To provide a single flat table (ml\_features) for machine learning training.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
To preserve all raw and derived data for future analysis without
having to re‑run experiments.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{UNITS AND SCIENTIFIC ASSUMPTIONS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All times are stored in nanoseconds (ns) for integer precision.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All energies are stored in microjoules (µJ) to avoid floating‑point drift.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Power is stored in watts (J/s) as REAL.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Frequencies in MHz (megahertz), temperatures in °C.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Baseline correction: dynamic\_energy = total\_energy \sphinxhyphen{} idle\_baseline\_energy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Orchestration tax = agentic\_dynamic \sphinxhyphen{} linear\_dynamic (paired runs).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tax is distributed proportionally to each event’s energy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All derived columns (energy\_per\_instruction, etc.) are computed in Python
to ensure correctness and are stored for ML convenience.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
We assume that linear and agentic runs are paired by \sphinxtitleref{run\_number} within
the same experiment.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{WHAT IT MUST NEVER BREAK:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Must never lose data: all inserts are transactional.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Must never corrupt foreign key relationships.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Must never insert duplicate runs (enforced by unique index).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Must never miscalculate tax due to missing pairs.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Main interface to the A‑LEMS SQLite database.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles all interactions with the persistent storage:
\sphinxhyphen{} Creating tables and indexes.
\sphinxhyphen{} Inserting experiments, runs, events, samples.
\sphinxhyphen{} Computing tax summaries and run state hashes.
\sphinxhyphen{} Providing query methods for GUI and ML.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All insert operations are transactional to ensure consistency.
Units are strictly enforced: times in ns, energies in µJ.

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{experiment\_meta}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{runs}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{List\DUrole{p}{{[}}Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hardware\_info}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }None}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ int}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Save a complete experiment with all its runs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the main entry point for storing results from the harness.
It performs the following steps:
1. Insert the experiment metadata.
2. If hardware\_info provided, insert/get hardware\_id.
3. Insert each run (with all metrics, events, samples).
4. After all runs are inserted, compute tax summaries by pairing
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
linear and agentic runs with the same run\_number.
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
All inserts happen inside a transaction to ensure atomicity.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{experiment\_meta}} \textendash{} Dictionary with keys:
\sphinxhyphen{} name (str): experiment name
\sphinxhyphen{} description (str): optional description
\sphinxhyphen{} workflow\_type (str): ‘linear’ or ‘agentic’
\sphinxhyphen{} model\_name (str)
\sphinxhyphen{} provider (str)
\sphinxhyphen{} task\_name (str)
\sphinxhyphen{} country\_code (str, default ‘US’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{runs}} \textendash{} List of run result dictionaries from the harness.
Each run dict must contain:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘ml\_features’ (dict with all the 77 fields)
\sphinxhyphen{} optionally ‘layer3\_derived’
\sphinxhyphen{} optionally ‘execution’
\sphinxhyphen{} optionally ‘sustainability’
\sphinxhyphen{} optionally ‘orchestration\_events’
\sphinxhyphen{} optionally ‘energy\_samples’, ‘cpu\_samples’, ‘interrupt\_samples’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hardware\_info}} \textendash{} Optional dict with hardware details.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
exp\_id of the newly inserted experiment.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\paragraph{core.database.schema module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.schema:module-core.database.schema}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.schema:core-database-schema-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.schema::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.schema@\spxentry{core.database.schema}}\index{core.database.schema@\spxentry{core.database.schema}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DATABASE SCHEMA \textendash{} SQL definitions for all A‑LEMS tables}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.schema:database-schema-sql-definitions-for-all-alems-tables}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains all CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, and CREATE VIEW statements
for the A‑LEMS database. This file is pure SQL \textendash{} no Python logic.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Separates schema definition from database logic

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Makes schema changes easier to track

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Can be reused by migration scripts

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keeps SQL in one place for review

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{TABLES:}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
experiments

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
hardware\_config

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
idle\_baselines

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
runs (main table, 70+ columns)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
orchestration\_events

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
orchestration\_tax\_summary

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
energy\_samples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
cpu\_samples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
interrupt\_samples

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ml\_features (view)

\end{enumerate}

\end{description}


\subparagraph{AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.schema:author-deepak-panigrahy}}
\sphinxstepscope


\paragraph{core.database.sqlite\_adapter module}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.sqlite_adapter:module-core.database.sqlite_adapter}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.sqlite_adapter:core-database-sqlite-adapter-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.database.sqlite_adapter::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.database.sqlite\_adapter@\spxentry{core.database.sqlite\_adapter}}\index{core.database.sqlite\_adapter@\spxentry{core.database.sqlite\_adapter}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{SQLITE ADAPTER \textendash{} SQLite implementation of DatabaseInterface}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.sqlite_adapter:sqlite-adapter-sqlite-implementation-of-databaseinterface}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{PURPOSE:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements the DatabaseInterface for SQLite. This adapter handles all
SQLite\sphinxhyphen{}specific connection details, query execution, and data insertion.

\sphinxlineitem{WHY THIS EXISTS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Separates SQLite\sphinxhyphen{}specific code from the rest of the application

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Makes it easy to switch to PostgreSQL later

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centralizes all SQLite pragmas and settings

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements the adapter pattern for database flexibility

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{UNITS AND ASSUMPTIONS:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All time values are in nanoseconds

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All energy values are in microjoules

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
SQLite connection uses Row factory for dict\sphinxhyphen{}like access

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Foreign keys must be enabled for data integrity

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
WAL journal mode for better concurrency

\end{itemize}

\end{description}


\subparagraph{AUTHOR: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.database.sqlite_adapter:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{SQLiteAdapter (class in core.database.sqlite\_adapter)@\spxentry{SQLiteAdapter}\spxextra{class in core.database.sqlite\_adapter}}

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxhyphen{} Schema creation with IF NOT EXISTS

\paragraph{core.execution.agentic module}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:module-core.execution.agentic}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:core-execution-agentic-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.execution.agentic@\spxentry{core.execution.agentic}}\index{core.execution.agentic@\spxentry{core.execution.agentic}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{AGENTIC AI EXECUTOR \textendash{} Multi\sphinxhyphen{}step LLM with tool support}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:agentic-ai-executor-multi-step-llm-with-tool-support}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose: Implements agentic AI workflows to measure orchestration tax.}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plans tasks, uses tools, synthesizes results

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Phase\sphinxhyphen{}level timing for scientific analysis

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{SCIENTIFIC NOTES:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses SAME base prompt as linear for fair comparison

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Planning phase uses temperature=0 for reproducibility

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Phase timing separates planning/execution/synthesis

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Complexity score weights multiple factors with proper normalization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Both cloud (Groq) and local (Ollama) providers supported

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Requirements:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 3.1: Dual\sphinxhyphen{}Harness Support \textendash{} local/cloud via config
Req 3.2: Complexity\sphinxhyphen{}Level Logic \textendash{} based on tool calls
Req 3.6: Device Handoff Latency \textendash{} phase\sphinxhyphen{}level timing

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{AgenticExecutor (class in core.execution.agentic)@\spxentry{AgenticExecutor}\spxextra{class in core.execution.agentic}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:core.execution.agentic.AgenticExecutor}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.execution.agentic.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{AgenticExecutor}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model\_config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Executes agentic AI workflows with tool support.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Workflow:}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Planning Phase: LLM creates step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step execution plan

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execution Phase: Each step runs (tool or LLM)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Synthesis Phase: Combine all results into final answer

\end{enumerate}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of LLM calls = 1 (plan) + N (steps) + 1 (synthesis)
where N is the number of steps that require LLM reasoning.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All configuration comes from Module 0 \textendash{} no hardcoding.
Debug output controlled by A\_LEMS\_DEBUG environment variable.
\index{execute() (core.execution.agentic.AgenticExecutor method)@\spxentry{execute()}\spxextra{core.execution.agentic.AgenticExecutor method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.execution.agentic:core.execution.agentic.AgenticExecutor.execute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{execute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{task}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{str}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{planning\_temperature}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{float}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute agentic workflow with phase\sphinxhyphen{}level timing.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the main entry point that runs the complete agentic pipeline:
1. Planning: LLM creates step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step plan (temperature=0 for reproducibility)
2. Execution: Each step runs (tool or LLM)
3. Synthesis: Combine all results into final answer

\sphinxlineitem{Why this exists:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Measures energy consumption of agentic workflows (Req 3.6)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Determines complexity based on tool count (Req 3.2)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Phase timing reveals where orchestration tax is spent

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Results used to calculate overhead vs linear AI

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{task}} \textendash{} User query (e.g., “What is 2+2?”)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{planning\_temperature}} \textendash{} Temperature for planning phase (default 0.0 for reproducibility)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary with all metrics needed for energy analysis

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{BASE MODULE \textendash{} Shared utility functions for experiment execution}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.base:base-module-shared-utility-functions-for-experiment-execution}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains base classes and utility functions used across execution modules.
This is the first module extracted from harness.py during refactoring.

\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
calc\_stats(data) \textendash{} Calculate mean, std, and 95\% CI for experimental data

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy (refactored from harness.py)}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.base:author-deepak-panigrahy-refactored-from-harness-py}}\index{calc\_stats() (in module core.execution.base)@\spxentry{calc\_stats()}\spxextra{in module core.execution.base}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.execution.base:core.execution.base.calc_stats}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.execution.base.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calc\_stats}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{List\DUrole{p}{{[}}float\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }float\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate statistics for experimental data.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data}} \textendash{} List of numeric values (energies, times, taxes, etc.)

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
mean: Arithmetic mean

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
std: Sample standard deviation (n\sphinxhyphen{}1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ci\_lower: Lower bound of 95\% confidence interval

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ci\_upper: Upper bound of 95\% confidence interval

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
min: Minimum value

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
max: Maximum value

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
n: Number of samples

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary with

\end{description}\end{quote}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Scientific note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses Student’s t\sphinxhyphen{}distribution for CI when n\textgreater{}=2.
For n=1, returns mean only (CI = nan).
For n=0, returns zeros and nan.

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{EXPERIMENT RUNNER \textendash{} Shared logic for all experiment scripts}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.experiment_runner:experiment-runner-shared-logic-for-all-experiment-scripts}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module contains ONLY the code that is duplicated between test\_harness.py
and run\_experiment.py. All original features remain in each script.

\sphinxAtStartPar
NEW FEATURES ADDED:
\sphinxhyphen{} Session grouping (group\_id)
\sphinxhyphen{} Status tracking (running/completed/partial/failed)
\sphinxhyphen{} Multi\sphinxhyphen{}provider support
\sphinxhyphen{} Progress tracking (runs\_completed/runs\_total)

\paragraph{core.execution.harness module}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.harness:module-core.execution.harness}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.harness:core-execution-harness-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.harness::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.execution.harness@\spxentry{core.execution.harness}}\index{core.execution.harness@\spxentry{core.execution.harness}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{EXPERIMENT HARNESS \textendash{} Wraps AI execution with energy measurement}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.harness:experiment-harness-wraps-ai-execution-with-energy-measurement}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the CRITICAL synchronization layer between energy measurement
(Module 1) and AI execution (Module 3). It ensures perfect alignment
between RAPL counters and code execution.

\sphinxlineitem{SCIENTIFIC NOTES:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Uses Module 1’s 3‑layer architecture correctly:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Layer 1: RawEnergyMeasurement (from EnergyEngine)
Layer 2: BaselineMeasurement (idle power)
Layer 3: DerivedEnergyMeasurement (computed metrics for analysis)

\end{description}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ALL THREE LAYERS are returned in results for complete transparency

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Energy measurement wraps ENTIRE execution (not just API calls)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Warmup runs discard first execution to eliminate cache/initialization effects

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multiple repetitions (n\textgreater{}=30) for statistical power

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cool‑down period between linear and agentic ensures fair comparison

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Network latency tracked separately for cloud models

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
CPU metrics come from Layer 3’s performance counters

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Why this exists:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Timestamps inside executors can drift from hardware counters

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Even 50ms misalignment corrupts orchestration tax measurements

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This harness guarantees perfect synchronization

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Requirements:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 3.6: Device Handoff Latency \textendash{} precise timing alignment
Req 1.46: High‑frequency sampling \textendash{} must capture short agent steps
Req 3.5: Cool‑down period between runs

\end{description}

\paragraph{core.execution.linear module}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.linear:module-core.execution.linear}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.linear:core-execution-linear-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.execution.linear::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.execution.linear@\spxentry{core.execution.linear}}\index{core.execution.linear@\spxentry{core.execution.linear}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{LINEAR AI EXECUTOR \textendash{} Single LLM call, no tools}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.linear:linear-ai-executor-single-llm-call-no-tools}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose: Implements linear AI workflows as the baseline for measuring}
\sphinxAtStartPar
orchestration tax. Single LLM call with no tool usage.

\sphinxlineitem{Why this exists:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Linear AI is the CONTROL case for experiments. By comparing its energy
consumption against agentic AI, we can quantify the “orchestration tax” \textendash{}
the additional energy overhead of planning, tool use, and synthesis.

\sphinxlineitem{SCIENTIFIC NOTES:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
This executor uses a STANDARDIZED prompt format identical to agentic’s
base prompt to ensure fair comparison.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All timestamps are recorded for precise energy alignment.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Both cloud (Groq) and local (Ollama) providers are supported.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Requirements:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 3.1: Dual\sphinxhyphen{}Harness Support \textendash{} local/cloud via config
Req 3.6: Device Handoff Latency \textendash{} exact start/end timestamps

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.linear:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{LinearExecutor (class in core.execution.linear)@\spxentry{LinearExecutor}\spxextra{class in core.execution.linear}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Executes a single LLM call (linear workflow) with no tools.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the baseline case for orchestration tax experiments:
\sphinxhyphen{} One system prompt
\sphinxhyphen{} One user message
\sphinxhyphen{} One synchronous API call
\sphinxhyphen{} Direct answer \textendash{} no tool calls, no loops, no agent reasoning

\sphinxAtStartPar
Its energy profile is the reference against which agentic overhead is measured.
All configuration comes from Module 0 \textendash{} no hardcoding.
Debug output controlled by A\_LEMS\_DEBUG environment variable.

\subparagraph{OPTIMIZER WRAPPER \sphinxhyphen{} Runs agentic code with real\sphinxhyphen{}time system optimization}
\label{\detokenize{core.execution.optimizer_wrapper:optimizer-wrapper-runs-agentic-code-with-real-time-system-optimization}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This wrapper:
1. Launches the real agentic.py as a subprocess
2. Detects phases via environment variables or signals
3. Applies system optimizations based on YOUR A\sphinxhyphen{}LEMS data
4. Passes through all results unchanged

\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model\_config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Executes agentic AI workflows with tool support.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Workflow:}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Planning Phase: LLM creates step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step execution plan

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execution Phase: Each step runs (tool or LLM)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Synthesis Phase: Combine all results into final answer

\end{enumerate}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of LLM calls = 1 (plan) + N (steps) + 1 (synthesis)
where N is the number of steps that require LLM reasoning.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All configuration comes from Module 0 \textendash{} no hardcoding.
Debug output controlled by A\_LEMS\_DEBUG environment variable.
\index{execute() (core.execution.AgenticExecutor method)@\spxentry{execute()}\spxextra{core.execution.AgenticExecutor method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.execution:core.execution.AgenticExecutor.execute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{execute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{task}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{str}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{planning\_temperature}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{float}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Execute agentic workflow with phase\sphinxhyphen{}level timing.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Purpose:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the main entry point that runs the complete agentic pipeline:
1. Planning: LLM creates step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step plan (temperature=0 for reproducibility)
2. Execution: Each step runs (tool or LLM)
3. Synthesis: Combine all results into final answer

\sphinxlineitem{Why this exists:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Measures energy consumption of agentic workflows (Req 3.6)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Determines complexity based on tool count (Req 3.2)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Phase timing reveals where orchestration tax is spent

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Results used to calculate overhead vs linear AI

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{task}} \textendash{} User query (e.g., “What is 2+2?”)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{planning\_temperature}} \textendash{} Temperature for planning phase (default 0.0 for reproducibility)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary with all metrics needed for energy analysis

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{min\_power\_watts (core.models.baseline\_measurement.BaselineMeasurement property)@\spxentry{min\_power\_watts}\spxextra{core.models.baseline\_measurement.BaselineMeasurement property}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.models.baseline_measurement:core.models.baseline_measurement.BaselineMeasurement.min_power_watts}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{property}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{min\_power\_watts}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }float\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Estimate a lower\sphinxhyphen{}bound idle power for realistic baseline.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Math: mean \sphinxhyphen{} 2*std\_dev ≈ 2nd percentile (assuming normal distribution)
\sphinxhyphen{} 68\% of values fall within mean \(\pm\) 1σ
\sphinxhyphen{} 95\% of values fall within mean \(\pm\) 2σ
\sphinxhyphen{} 2.5\% of values are below mean \sphinxhyphen{} 2σ
\sphinxhyphen{} So mean \sphinxhyphen{} 2σ represents \textasciitilde{}2.5th percentile (conservative low bound)

\sphinxAtStartPar
For non\sphinxhyphen{}normal distributions, this still gives a robust lower estimate
that eliminates most background noise while staying physically possible.

\end{fulllineitems}

\paragraph{core.models.derived\_energy\_measurement module}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.derived_energy_measurement:module-core.models.derived_energy_measurement}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.derived_energy_measurement:core-models-derived-energy-measurement-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.derived_energy_measurement::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.models.derived\_energy\_measurement@\spxentry{core.models.derived\_energy\_measurement}}\index{core.models.derived\_energy\_measurement@\spxentry{core.models.derived\_energy\_measurement}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DERIVED ENERGY MEASUREMENT \textendash{} Layer 3: Analysis Results}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.derived_energy_measurement:derived-energy-measurement-layer-3-analysis-results}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class represents computed/derived metrics from raw data and baseline.
NEVER stored permanently \textendash{} computed on demand when needed.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes ALL 25 hardware parameters from Module 1:
\sphinxhyphen{} RAPL energy (package, core, dram, uncore)
\sphinxhyphen{} Performance counters (instructions, cycles, cache, context switches)
\sphinxhyphen{} Power states (C\sphinxhyphen{}state residencies, frequencies)
\sphinxhyphen{} Thermal data (temperatures)
\sphinxhyphen{} Scheduler metrics (run queue, context switches)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Layer 3 \textendash{} Computed on demand, never stored permanently.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Contains ALL 25 hardware parameters from Module 1 for complete analysis.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Energy Attributes:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
measurement\_id: Reference to raw measurement
baseline\_id: Optional reference to baseline used

\sphinxAtStartPar
package\_energy\_uj: Total package energy (raw)
core\_energy\_uj: Core energy (raw)
dram\_energy\_uj: DRAM energy if available (raw)

\sphinxAtStartPar
uncore\_energy\_uj: Uncore waste (package \sphinxhyphen{} core \sphinxhyphen{} dram)
idle\_energy\_uj: Estimated idle energy from baseline
workload\_energy\_uj: Energy attributed to workload (package \sphinxhyphen{} idle)
reasoning\_energy\_uj: Energy for actual computation (core \sphinxhyphen{} idle\_core)
orchestration\_tax\_uj: Energy for overhead (uncore + extra)

\sphinxlineitem{Performance Counters (Req 1.5, 1.6, 1.10):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
instructions: int = 0                  \# Instructions retired
cycles: int = 0                         \# CPU cycles
cache\_misses: int = 0                    \# LLC cache misses
cache\_references: int = 0                 \# LLC references
ipc: float = 0.0                          \# Instructions per cycle
page\_faults: int = 0                       \# Major + minor page faults

\sphinxlineitem{Power States (Req 1.7, 1.41, 1.8):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
c\_state\_residencies: Dict{[}str, float{]} = None  \# C1\%, C6\%, C7\%, etc.
frequency\_mhz: float = 0.0                     \# CPU frequency
gpu\_frequency\_mhz: float = 0.0                  \# iGPU frequency
gpu\_rc6\_percent: float = 0.0                     \# iGPU RC6 residency

\sphinxlineitem{Thermal (Req 1.9):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
package\_temp\_celsius: float = 0.0                \# Package temperature
core\_temps\_celsius: List{[}float{]} = None           \# Per\sphinxhyphen{}core temperatures

\sphinxlineitem{Scheduler (Req 1.12, 1.23, 1.36):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
context\_switches\_voluntary: int = 0              \# Voluntary context switches
context\_switches\_involuntary: int = 0            \# Involuntary context switches
run\_queue\_length: float = 0.0                     \# Run queue length
kernel\_time\_ms: float = 0.0                       \# Time in kernel mode
user\_time\_ms: float = 0.0                         \# Time in user mode

\sphinxlineitem{Duration:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
duration\_seconds: float

\end{description}

\paragraph{core.models.energy\_measurement module}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.energy_measurement:module-core.models.energy_measurement}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.energy_measurement:core-models-energy-measurement-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.energy_measurement::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.models.energy\_measurement@\spxentry{core.models.energy\_measurement}}\index{core.models.energy\_measurement@\spxentry{core.models.energy\_measurement}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{ENERGY MEASUREMENT DATA MODELS}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.energy_measurement:energy-measurement-data-models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the data structures used throughout Module 1 to represent
energy measurements, performance counters, and hardware state.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All data classes are designed to be:
\sphinxhyphen{} Self\sphinxhyphen{}documenting (field names explain their purpose)
\sphinxhyphen{} Serializable (can be saved to JSON/database)
\sphinxhyphen{} Type\sphinxhyphen{}safe (using dataclasses with type hints)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Complete energy measurement result from a single experiment run.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the primary output of Module 1. It contains everything
needed for sustainability calculations (Module 2) and orchestration
tax analysis (Module 3).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The object is designed to be:
\sphinxhyphen{} Self\sphinxhyphen{}contained (all data in one place)
\sphinxhyphen{} Serializable (can be saved to database)
\sphinxhyphen{} Comparable (can compare linear vs agentic runs)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.1 through 1.47 are all represented here.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hardware performance counter readings from perf\_events.

\sphinxAtStartPar
These counters provide insight into what the CPU was actually doing
during the measurement interval. They help distinguish between:
\sphinxhyphen{} Actual computation (instructions retired)
\sphinxhyphen{} Memory bottlenecks (cache misses)
\sphinxhyphen{} OS scheduler overhead (context switches)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.5: Instruction density (IPC)
Req 1.6: Cache behavior
Req 1.10: Page faults
Req 1.43: Thread migrations

\sphinxhyphen{} High IPC (\textgreater{}1) means CPU was doing useful work

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
CPU power state information including frequencies and C\sphinxhyphen{}states.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Modern CPUs save power by entering deeper sleep states (C\sphinxhyphen{}states)
and lower frequencies (P\sphinxhyphen{}states). This data helps attribute energy
to actual work vs. waiting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.4: DVFS attribution (frequency)
Req 1.7: C\sphinxhyphen{}state residency
Req 1.8: iGPU states
Req 1.41: Core\sphinxhyphen{}specific C\sphinxhyphen{}states

\paragraph{core.models.performance\_counters module}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.performance_counters:module-core.models.performance_counters}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.performance_counters:core-models-performance-counters-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.models.performance_counters::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.models.performance\_counters@\spxentry{core.models.performance\_counters}}\index{core.models.performance\_counters@\spxentry{core.models.performance\_counters}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{PERFORMANCE COUNTERS DATA MODEL}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.performance_counters:performance-counters-data-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the PerformanceCounters data class used throughout Module 1
to store hardware performance counter readings.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class provides:
\sphinxhyphen{} Type\sphinxhyphen{}safe fields for all counter types
\sphinxhyphen{} Derived metrics (IPC, cache miss rate)
\sphinxhyphen{} Serialization methods

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hardware performance counter readings from perf\_events.

\sphinxAtStartPar
These counters provide insight into what the CPU was actually doing
during the measurement interval. They help distinguish between:
\sphinxhyphen{} Actual computation (instructions retired)
\sphinxhyphen{} Memory bottlenecks (cache misses)
\sphinxhyphen{} OS scheduler overhead (context switches)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.5: Instruction density (IPC)
Req 1.6: Cache behavior
Req 1.10: Page faults
Req 1.43: Thread migrations

{}
{{ $\rightarrow$ float}}
\pysigstopsignatures

Calculate Instructions Per Cycle (IPC).

\sphinxAtStartPar
IPC tells us how efficiently the CPU was running:
\sphinxhyphen{} High IPC (\textgreater{}1) means CPU was doing useful work
\sphinxhyphen{} Low IPC (\textless{}0.5) suggests stalls (waiting for memory)

\sphinxAtStartPar

\subparagraph{RAW ENERGY MEASUREMENT \textendash{} Layer 1: Ground Truth}
\label{\detokenize{core.models.raw_energy_measurement:raw-energy-measurement-layer-1-ground-truth}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class represents pure raw measurement data from hardware counters.
NEVER modified after creation. Contains only raw readings, no corrections.

\paragraph{core.readers.msr\_reader module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.msr_reader:module-core.readers.msr_reader}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.msr_reader:core-readers-msr-reader-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.msr_reader::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.msr\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.msr\_reader}}\index{core.readers.msr\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.msr\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{MSR READER \sphinxhyphen{} Direct MSR Access with C Helper and TSC Conversion}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.msr_reader:msr-reader-direct-msr-access-with-c-helper-and-tsc-conversion}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Deepak Panigrahy

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides MSR access using a privileged C helper binary.
The C binary has CAP\_SYS\_RAWIO set, so Python can run without sudo.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Uses C helper binary (no sudo needed for Python)
\sphinxhyphen{} Falls back to direct access if helper unavailable
\sphinxhyphen{} Reads TSC frequency from hw\_config.json for accurate C\sphinxhyphen{}state conversion
\sphinxhyphen{} Converts C\sphinxhyphen{}state counters from TSC ticks to real time (seconds/microseconds)
\sphinxhyphen{} CPU pinning for consistent measurements
\sphinxhyphen{} Baseline loading (stable hardware properties)
\sphinxhyphen{} Dynamic measurement methods for per‑run counters

\sphinxAtStartPar
Requirements Covered:
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.21: Ring Bus Frequency
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.27: C\sphinxhyphen{}State Transition Counters (now properly converted to time)
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.47: Wake\sphinxhyphen{}up Latency
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.9: Thermal Throttling


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip

{{ $\rightarrow$ float\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }None}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate actual CPU frequency from APERF/MPERF deltas.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Formula: actual\_freq = TSC\_freq * (ΔAPERF / ΔMPERF)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{SCIENTIFIC NOTES:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ratio should be between 0 and 5 for valid CPU frequencies

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles 64\sphinxhyphen{}bit counter wrap\sphinxhyphen{}around

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns None if calculation is invalid

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{aperf1}} \textendash{} APERF values at start and end

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{aperf2}} \textendash{} APERF values at start and end

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mperf1}} \textendash{} MPERF values at start and end

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mperf2}} \textendash{} MPERF values at start and end

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Actual frequency in MHz, or None if invalid

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

Calculate number of wakeups between two snapshots.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each time the CPU wakes from deep sleep (C6/C7), the counter increments.
By comparing start and end values, we can count total wakeups during
the experiment.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Safely extract a counter value, ensuring it’s a number.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use this method whenever accessing C\sphinxhyphen{}state counters to avoid NoneType errors.
This provides a single point of defense against None values, missing keys,
and type conversion issues.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{counters\_dict}} \textendash{} Dictionary possibly containing counter values

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Combines both package and core thermal status for accurate throttling detection.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{CRITICAL SCIENTIFIC NOTES:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If both MSR reads fail, returns None (skip this experiment)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If one read fails, use defaults (0,0) for that domain

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Throttling detection must use OR logic across domains

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Use this at START and END of experiment to detect if throttling
occurred DURING the experiment (end\_log \textgreater{} start\_log).

\paragraph{core.readers.perf\_reader module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader:module-core.readers.perf_reader}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader:core-readers-perf-reader-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.perf\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.perf\_reader}}\index{core.readers.perf\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.perf\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{PERFORMANCE COUNTER READER \sphinxhyphen{} Linux perf\_events Interface}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader:performance-counter-reader-linux-perf-events-interface}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides access to hardware performance counters via the Linux
perf\_events interface. perf\_events allows reading CPU performance monitoring
unit (PMU) counters, which provide detailed insight into what the CPU is
actually doing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Why perf\_events matters for AI energy research:
\sphinxhyphen{} Instructions/cycle tells us if CPU is doing useful work or stalled
\sphinxhyphen{} Cache misses indicate memory bandwidth pressure from large models
\sphinxhyphen{} Context switches reveal OS scheduler overhead (orchestration tax)
\sphinxhyphen{} Page faults show when models don’t fit in RAM

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reference: perf\_event\_open(2) man page, Linux kernel documentation


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{PerfReader (class in core.readers.perf\_reader)@\spxentry{PerfReader}\spxextra{class in core.readers.perf\_reader}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reads hardware performance counters using Linux perf\_events.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides access to CPU performance counters via the ‘perf stat’
command. It parses the output and returns structured PerformanceCounters
objects.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The reader handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Checking perf availability and permissions
\sphinxhyphen{} Running perf stat for specified durations
\sphinxhyphen{} Parsing perf output reliably
\sphinxhyphen{} Graceful degradation if perf is not available

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.5: Instructions, cycles, IPC
Req 1.6: Cache references and misses
Req 1.10: Page faults
Req 1.43: Thread migrations

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{check\_paranoid\_setting}}}
{}
{{ $\rightarrow$ int\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }None}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Check the current kernel.perf\_event\_paranoid setting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The paranoid setting controls access to perf events:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxhyphen{}1: No restrictions (recommended for research)
\sphinxhyphen{} 0: Allow access to CPU\sphinxhyphen{}specific data
\sphinxhyphen{} 1: Allow kernel and user\sphinxhyphen{}space measurements
\sphinxhyphen{} 2: Allow only user\sphinxhyphen{}space measurements (default on many systems)
\sphinxhyphen{} 3: Disable all perf events
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Current paranoid value, or None if cannot read

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{read\_counters() (core.readers.perf\_reader.PerfReader method)@\spxentry{read\_counters()}\spxextra{core.readers.perf\_reader.PerfReader method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.readers.perf_reader:core.readers.perf_reader.PerfReader.read_counters}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_counters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{duration\_ms}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{int}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{100}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ {\hyperref[\detokenize{core.models.performance_counters:core.models.performance_counters.PerformanceCounters}]{\sphinxcrossref{PerformanceCounters}}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read performance counters for specified duration.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This runs ‘perf stat’ for the specified duration and parses
the output into a PerformanceCounters object.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{duration\_ms}} \textendash{} How long to sample (milliseconds)
Shorter durations give higher resolution but more noise.
100ms is a good balance for AI workloads.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PerformanceCounters object with all counter values.
Returns empty counters (all zeros) if perf is not available.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.5: Instructions, cycles, IPC
Req 1.6: Cache references and misses
Req 1.10: Page faults
Req 1.43: Thread migrations

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{reader} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{PerfReader}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{config}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{IPC: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{counters}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{instructions\PYGZus{}per\PYGZus{}cycle}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.2f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Read multiple samples over time.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is useful for capturing variation in performance counters
during longer experiments, such as tracking how IPC changes
across different phases of agentic AI execution.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{interval\_ms}} \textendash{} Time between samples in milliseconds

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{samples}} \textendash{} Number of samples to take

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of PerformanceCounters objects, one per sample

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Monitor performance every 50ms for 1 second}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{samples} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{reader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{read\PYGZus{}interval\PYGZus{}counters}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{interval\PYGZus{}ms}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{50}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{samples}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Sample }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{i}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{: IPC=}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{c}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{instructions\PYGZus{}per\PYGZus{}cycle}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{l+s+si}{:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{.2f}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\paragraph{core.readers.rapl\_reader module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.rapl_reader:module-core.readers.rapl_reader}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.rapl_reader:core-readers-rapl-reader-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.rapl_reader::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.rapl\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.rapl\_reader}}\index{core.readers.rapl\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.rapl\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{RAPL ENERGY READER \sphinxhyphen{} Intel Running Average Power Limit Interface}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.rapl_reader:rapl-energy-reader-intel-running-average-power-limit-interface}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides access to Intel RAPL (Running Average Power Limit) energy
counters. RAPL is a hardware feature that provides energy consumption estimates
for different power domains within the CPU.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Why RAPL over external power meters:
\sphinxhyphen{} Microsecond\sphinxhyphen{}scale sampling possible
\sphinxhyphen{} Per\sphinxhyphen{}domain breakdown (critical for orchestration tax attribution)
\sphinxhyphen{} Available on all modern Intel/AMD systems
\sphinxhyphen{} No additional hardware required

\sphinxAtStartPar
RAPL provides energy in microjoules since system boot (cumulative counter).
To measure energy over an interval:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read counter at start (E1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read counter at end (E2)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Energy = E2 \sphinxhyphen{} E1 (handling wrap\sphinxhyphen{}around)

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reference: Intel Software Developer’s Manual, Volume 3, Chapter 14.9


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.rapl_reader:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{RAPLReader (class in core.readers.rapl\_reader)@\spxentry{RAPLReader}\spxextra{class in core.readers.rapl\_reader}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.readers.rapl\_reader.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{RAPLReader}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reads Intel RAPL (Running Average Power Limit) energy counters from sysfs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles the low\sphinxhyphen{}level interaction with the RAPL interface,
including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Detecting available domains via name files
\sphinxhyphen{} Reading energy values with retry logic
\sphinxhyphen{} Handling permission issues gracefully
\sphinxhyphen{} Supporting high\sphinxhyphen{}frequency sampling

\sphinxAtStartPar
The reader is designed to be used by the EnergyEngine, not directly
by end users.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 1.1: RAPL energy measurement
Req 1.3: Uncore waste calculation
Req 1.46: High\sphinxhyphen{}frequency sampling support

\begin{fulllineitems}

{}
{{ $\rightarrow$ Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }int\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read current energy values from all available RAPL domains.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method reads the cumulative energy counters from sysfs.
The values are in microjoules and increase monotonically until
they wrap around (typically after \textasciitilde{}2\textasciicircum{}32 µJ, about 70 minutes on a 100W CPU).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary mapping domain names to energy in microjoules.
Example: \{‘package’: 12345678, ‘core’: 8765432, ‘uncore’: 3580246\}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{IOError}} \textendash{} If a previously available path becomes inaccessible

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{{ $\rightarrow$ Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }int\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures

Read energy with retry logic for transient failures.

(may be zeros if all retries fail)

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{read\_with\_retry}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_retries}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{int}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{3}}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Read energy with retry logic for transient failures.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Occasional read failures can happen due to kernel contention.
This method retries a few times before giving up.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_retries}} \textendash{} Maximum number of retry attempts

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary of energy readings (may be partial if all retries fail)

\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If all retries fail, returns empty dict and logs error.
This is better than crashing the entire experiment.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\paragraph{core.readers.scheduler\_monitor module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor:module-core.readers.scheduler_monitor}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor:core-readers-scheduler-monitor-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.scheduler\_monitor@\spxentry{core.readers.scheduler\_monitor}}\index{core.readers.scheduler\_monitor@\spxentry{core.readers.scheduler\_monitor}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{SCHEDULER MONITOR \textendash{} Reads Linux scheduler statistics from /proc}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor:scheduler-monitor-reads-linux-scheduler-statistics-from-proc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module reads various scheduler and system metrics from the /proc filesystem:
\sphinxhyphen{} Context switches (voluntary/involuntary) from /proc/self/status
\sphinxhyphen{} Kernel and user CPU times from /proc/stat
\sphinxhyphen{} Run queue length from /proc/loadavg
\sphinxhyphen{} Thread migrations from /proc/self/status (if available)

\sphinxAtStartPar
These metrics help quantify OS‑level overhead that contributes to the
orchestration tax in agentic AI workflows.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Requirements implemented:
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.12: Kernel Context Switches
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.23: Kernel/User Ratio
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.24: Voluntary Switch Delay (proxy)
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.36: Run Queue Length


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{SchedulerMonitor (class in core.readers.scheduler\_monitor)@\spxentry{SchedulerMonitor}\spxextra{class in core.readers.scheduler\_monitor}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.readers.scheduler_monitor:core.readers.scheduler_monitor.SchedulerMonitor}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.readers.scheduler\_monitor.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{SchedulerMonitor}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reads Linux scheduler metrics from /proc filesystem.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides methods to read:
\sphinxhyphen{} Voluntary and involuntary context switches (Req 1.12)
\sphinxhyphen{} Kernel and user CPU times (Req 1.23)
\sphinxhyphen{} Run queue length (Req 1.36)
\sphinxhyphen{} Thread migrations (optional, can be derived from perf\_reader)

\sphinxAtStartPar
All values are read at a single point in time. For rates or deltas,
the caller should take two readings and compute differences.

\paragraph{core.readers.sensor\_reader module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.sensor_reader:module-core.readers.sensor_reader}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.sensor_reader:core-readers-sensor-reader-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.sensor_reader::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.sensor\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.sensor\_reader}}\index{core.readers.sensor\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.sensor\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{SENSOR READER \sphinxhyphen{} Temperature and Voltage Sensors from Sysfs}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.sensor_reader:sensor-reader-temperature-and-voltage-sensors-from-sysfs}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module reads temperature and voltage sensors from the Linux sysfs interface.
Unlike turbostat which reads CPU\sphinxhyphen{}internal sensors, this reads motherboard and
package sensors exposed by kernel drivers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Requirements implemented in this module:
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.9: Package thermal jitter (temperature monitoring)
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.2: Motherboard voltages (voltage monitoring)
\sphinxhyphen{} Req 1.38: System Agent voltage (VSA monitoring)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Sources:
\sphinxhyphen{} CPU package temperature: /sys/class/thermal/thermal\_zone*/temp
\sphinxhyphen{} Core temperatures: Multiple thermal zones
\sphinxhyphen{} Voltages: /sys/class/hwmon/hwmon*/in*\_input (if available)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Why this matters for AI energy research:
\sphinxhyphen{} Thermal throttling detection (Req 1.9)
\sphinxhyphen{} Correlation between temperature and energy consumption
\sphinxhyphen{} Validation that cooling is sufficient between runs


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}

\paragraph{core.readers.turbostat\_reader module}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.turbostat_reader:module-core.readers.turbostat_reader}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.turbostat_reader:core-readers-turbostat-reader-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.readers.turbostat_reader::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.readers.turbostat\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.turbostat\_reader}}\index{core.readers.turbostat\_reader@\spxentry{core.readers.turbostat\_reader}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{TURBOSTAT READER \sphinxhyphen{} Clean Implementation Using pandas}
\label{\detokenize{core.readers.turbostat_reader:turbostat-reader-clean-implementation-using-pandas}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Deepak Panigrahy

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a clean, robust reader for Intel turbostat output.
turbostat reads CPU MSRs to provide detailed performance data including:
\sphinxhyphen{} C\sphinxhyphen{}state residency percentages (time spent in idle states)
\sphinxhyphen{} CPU frequency during execution
\sphinxhyphen{} iGPU RC6 residency and frequency
\sphinxhyphen{} Package temperature
\sphinxhyphen{} Power estimates for package, cores, GPU, RAM

\sphinxAtStartPar
Requirements Covered:
Req 1.4 \textendash{} DVFS Attribution (frequencies)
Req 1.7 \textendash{} C\sphinxhyphen{}State Residency (via turbostat)
Req 1.8 \textendash{} iGPU RC6 \& GT Freq
Req 1.9 \textendash{} Package Thermal Jitter (temperature)
Req 1.41 \textendash{} Deepest Core C\sphinxhyphen{}State (via residency columns)
================================================================================
\index{TurbostatReader (class in core.readers.turbostat\_reader)@\spxentry{TurbostatReader}\spxextra{class in core.readers.turbostat\_reader}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.readers.turbostat_reader:core.readers.turbostat_reader.TurbostatReader}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.readers.turbostat\_reader.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TurbostatReader}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reads Intel turbostat metrics using pandas for clean parsing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Starting continuous monitoring of turbostat
\sphinxhyphen{} Draining output in background thread to prevent pipe buffer full
\sphinxhyphen{} Stopping monitoring and collecting all samples
\sphinxhyphen{} Parsing tab\sphinxhyphen{}separated output into pandas DataFrame
\sphinxhyphen{} Computing summary statistics (mean, std, min, max, median)
\sphinxhyphen{} Mapping columns to our internal PowerState object
\sphinxhyphen{} Capturing CPU topology for hybrid processors (P\sphinxhyphen{}cores/E\sphinxhyphen{}cores)
\sphinxhyphen{} Recording turbostat version for reproducibility

\sphinxAtStartPar
The reader uses column mappings from hw\_config.json, which are
detected automatically by Module 0’s hardware detection.

\subparagraph{CARBON METRICS \textendash{} Data classes for carbon footprint}
\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.models.carbon_metrics:carbon-metrics-data-classes-for-carbon-footprint}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines data structures for carbon emissions with:
\sphinxhyphen{} Uncertainty quantification {[}Req 2.4{]}
\sphinxhyphen{} Confidence intervals
\sphinxhyphen{} Source attribution {[}Req 2.1{]}
\sphinxhyphen{} Time\sphinxhyphen{}aware factors (future)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Requirements Covered:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 2.1: Country\sphinxhyphen{}Specific Carbon Intensity
Req 2.4: Total Operational Carbon (CCI)
Req 2.9: Carbon Receipt Per Task
Req 2.16: Global Metadata Integrity

\end{description}

Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Carbon footprint metrics for a single measurement with uncertainty.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Scientific Notes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Carbon intensity varies by grid region and time of day

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uncertainty reflects grid reporting accuracy (\(\pm\)5\sphinxhyphen{}15\%)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
95\% confidence intervals are standard for GHG reporting

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}query metrics require query\_count validation

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Carbon footprint metrics for a single measurement with uncertainty.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Scientific Notes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Carbon intensity varies by grid region and time of day

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uncertainty reflects grid reporting accuracy (\(\pm\)5\sphinxhyphen{}15\%)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
95\% confidence intervals are standard for GHG reporting

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}query metrics require query\_count validation

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Time\sphinxhyphen{}aware grid factors for future enhancement.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Carbon intensity varies by time of day due to:
\sphinxhyphen{} Solar generation (day/night)
\sphinxhyphen{} Wind patterns
\sphinxhyphen{} Grid demand

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides infrastructure for time\sphinxhyphen{}aware calculations.

\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.calculator:module-core.sustainability.calculator}}\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.calculator:core-sustainability-calculator-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.calculator::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.sustainability.calculator@\spxentry{core.sustainability.calculator}}\index{core.sustainability.calculator@\spxentry{core.sustainability.calculator}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{SUSTAINABILITY CALCULATOR \textendash{} Module 2 Main Entry Point}
\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.calculator:sustainability-calculator-module-2-main-entry-point}}

\subparagraph{Requirements Covered:}
\label{\detokenize{core.sustainability.calculator:requirements-covered}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 2.1: Country\sphinxhyphen{}Specific Carbon Intensity
Req 2.2: Country\sphinxhyphen{}Specific Water Intensity
Req 2.3: Methane Leakage Factor
Req 2.4: Total Operational Carbon (CCI)
Req 2.5: Total Water Consumption (WCI)
Req 2.6: Methane Emission Impact (MCI)
Req 2.7: Energy Per Query (EPQ)
Req 2.8: Wait\sphinxhyphen{}Tax Per Query (TPQ)
Req 2.9: Carbon Receipt Per Task
Req 2.10: Water Receipt Per Task
Req 2.12: Geographic Arbitrage Potential
Req 2.13: Energy Scarcity Index (ESI)
Req 2.16: Global Metadata Integrity


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Main sustainability calculator.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Transforms energy measurements into environmental impacts using
country\sphinxhyphen{}specific grid factors from Module 0.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Requirements:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Req 2.1\sphinxhyphen{}2.3: Grid intensity factors
Req 2.4\sphinxhyphen{}2.6: Environmental impacts
Req 2.7\sphinxhyphen{}2.10: Per\sphinxhyphen{}query metrics
Req 2.12\sphinxhyphen{}2.13: Geographic context
Req 2.16: Data integrity

\end{description}
