\paragraph{core.utils.core\_pinner module}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner:module-core.utils.core_pinner}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner:core-utils-core-pinner-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.utils.core\_pinner@\spxentry{core.utils.core\_pinner}}\index{core.utils.core\_pinner@\spxentry{core.utils.core\_pinner}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{CORE PINNER UTILITY \textendash{} CPU Affinity Management}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner:core-pinner-utility-cpu-affinity-management}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides CPU core pinning functionality to ensure consistent
measurements by preventing thread migration across cores (Req 1.15).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Pin current process to specific cores
\sphinxhyphen{} Get current affinity
\sphinxhyphen{} Support both default and runtime core specifications
\sphinxhyphen{} Cross\sphinxhyphen{}platform (Linux, macOS fallback)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
from core.utils.core\_pinner import CorePinner

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# With default cores
pinner = CorePinner(default\_cores={[}0, 1{]})
pinner.pin\_to\_cores()  \# Uses default

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Or specify at runtime
pinner.pin\_to\_cores({[}2, 3{]})  \# Override default

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get current affinity
current = pinner.get\_current\_affinity()

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{CorePinner (class in core.utils.core\_pinner)@\spxentry{CorePinner}\spxextra{class in core.utils.core\_pinner}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.utils.core_pinner:core.utils.core_pinner.CorePinner}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.utils.core\_pinner.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{CorePinner}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{default\_cores}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{List\DUrole{p}{{[}}int\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }None}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Manages CPU core affinity for consistent measurements.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides methods to pin the current process to specific
CPU cores, preventing the OS scheduler from migrating threads and
causing measurement noise (Req 1.15).
\index{get\_current\_affinity() (core.utils.core\_pinner.CorePinner method)@\spxentry{get\_current\_affinity()}\spxextra{core.utils.core\_pinner.CorePinner method}}

\pysigstopsignatures

Get the current CPU affinity for this process.

\paragraph{core.utils.debug module}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.debug:module-core.utils.debug}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.debug:core-utils-debug-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.debug::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.utils.debug@\spxentry{core.utils.debug}}\index{core.utils.debug@\spxentry{core.utils.debug}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DEBUG UTILITY \sphinxhyphen{} Unified debugging across all A\sphinxhyphen{}LEMS modules}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.debug:debug-utility-unified-debugging-across-all-a-lems-modules}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a consistent debugging interface. Debug output can be
enabled globally or per\sphinxhyphen{}module via environment variables.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage in your code:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
from core.utils.debug import dprint, set\_debug

\sphinxAtStartPar
dprint(“Reading MSR…”, msr=0x60d, value=12345)  \# Only prints if debug on

\sphinxlineitem{Environment variables:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
export A\_LEMS\_DEBUG=1           \# Enable ALL debug output
export A\_LEMS\_DEBUG\_MODULES=msr\_reader,rapl\_reader  \# Specific modules only
export A\_LEMS\_DEBUG\_FILE=/tmp/a\sphinxhyphen{}lems\sphinxhyphen{}debug.log  \# Log to file (optional)

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}

Measure system idle energy baseline using research\sphinxhyphen{}grade methodology.
Automatically caches the result and reuses it on subsequent calls.

\sphinxAtStartPar
NEW FEATURES:

\sphinxhyphen{} Returns proper BaselineMeasurement object with metadata
\sphinxhyphen{} Cache validation ensures state matches before reuse

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{num\_samples}} \textendash{} Number of samples to take

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pre\_wait\_seconds}} \textendash{} Time to wait before starting

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pin\_cores}} \textendash{} List of cores to pin to (None = use pinner’s default)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cache\_file}} \textendash{} Path to cache file (default: data/idle\_baseline.json)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_remeasure}} \textendash{} If True, ignore cache and force new measurement

\end{itemize}

\paragraph{core.utils.sampling module}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:module-core.utils.sampling}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:core-utils-sampling-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.utils.sampling@\spxentry{core.utils.sampling}}\index{core.utils.sampling@\spxentry{core.utils.sampling}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{HIGH\sphinxhyphen{}FREQUENCY SAMPLING UTILITY}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:high-frequency-sampling-utility}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Author: Deepak Panigrahy
Institution:
Contact:

\sphinxAtStartPar
Module: core/utils/sampling.py (Part of Module 1 \sphinxhyphen{} Energy Engine)


\subparagraph{Purpose:}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:purpose}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a high\sphinxhyphen{}frequency sampling thread that reads RAPL energy
counters at a configurable rate (default 100 Hz). This enables:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Capturing energy consumption during short agent steps (Req 1.46)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Attributing energy to specific phases of agentic workflows

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detecting energy spikes from context switches or tool calls

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validating that sampling frequency is sufficient (Req 1.46)

\end{enumerate}


\subparagraph{Why a Dedicated Sampling Thread:}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:why-a-dedicated-sampling-thread}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
RAPL counters are cumulative; we need differences over short intervals.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A background thread ensures consistent sampling without blocking the main
experiment code.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Samples are stored with timestamps for later correlation with workflow phases.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Requirements Covered:}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:requirements-covered}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Req 1.46 \textendash{} RAPL Sampling Frequency Validation \textendash{} ensures we capture enough}
\sphinxAtStartPar
samples for short agent steps (default 100 Hz = 10ms intervals).

\end{description}


\subparagraph{Design:}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:design}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The sampler runs in a daemon thread so it exits when the main program exits.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Samples are stored in a thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe queue to avoid locking issues.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The main thread can retrieve samples after the measurement stops.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage Example:}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.sampling:usage-example}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
sampler = HighFrequencySampler(rapl\_reader, sampling\_rate\_hz=100)
sampler.start()
\# … run experiment …
sampler.stop()
samples = sampler.get\_samples()

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Or get samples since a specific timestamp
recent = sampler.get\_samples\_since(start\_time)
\end{quote}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip

High\sphinxhyphen{}frequency RAPL sampling thread.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class runs a background thread that reads RAPL energy counters at a
specified rate (default 100 Hz = every 10ms). Samples are stored in a
thread\sphinxhyphen{}safe queue for later retrieval.

\subparagraph{VALIDATORS \textendash{} Check measurement quality (Req 1.46, Req 1.9)}
\label{\detokenize{core.utils.validators:validators-check-measurement-quality-req-1-46-req-1-9}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides functions to validate energy measurements for quality issues such as:
\sphinxhyphen{} Negative energy (should not happen)
\sphinxhyphen{} Thermal throttling (high temperature)
\sphinxhyphen{} Too few samples
\sphinxhyphen{} Zero energy

\subsection{core.energy\_engine module}
\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine:module-core.energy_engine}}\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine:core-energy-engine-module}}\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!core.energy\_engine@\spxentry{core.energy\_engine}}\index{core.energy\_engine@\spxentry{core.energy\_engine}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subsubsection{ENERGY ENGINE \textendash{} Main Orchestrator for Module 1}
\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine:energy-engine-main-orchestrator-for-module-1}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module ties together all hardware readers and provides a clean interface
for measuring energy consumption during AI workloads. It is designed to be used
as a context manager or directly via start/stop methods.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The EnergyEngine handles:
\sphinxhyphen{} Initializing all readers (RAPL, perf, turbostat, sensors, MSR)
\sphinxhyphen{} Core pinning for consistent measurements (Req 1.15)
\sphinxhyphen{} Idle baseline measurement using research‑grade utility (Req 1.45)
\sphinxhyphen{} High‑frequency sampling (Req 1.46)
\sphinxhyphen{} Coordinated start/stop of all readers
\sphinxhyphen{} Assembling a complete RawEnergyMeasurement object
\sphinxhyphen{} Validation of measurement quality

\sphinxAtStartPar
IMPORTANT: This engine returns RAW measurements only.
Baseline correction happens in the analysis layer (EnergyAnalyzer).

\sphinxAtStartPar
All paths and settings come from Module 0’s configuration (hw\_config.json,
app\_settings.yaml). No hardcoding.


\paragraph{Author: Deepak Panigrahy}
\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine:author-deepak-panigrahy}}\index{EnergyEngine (class in core.energy\_engine)@\spxentry{EnergyEngine}\spxextra{class in core.energy\_engine}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{core.energy_engine:core.energy_engine.EnergyEngine}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{core.energy\_engine.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{EnergyEngine}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{config}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{Dict\DUrole{p}{{[}}str\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }Any\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Main energy measurement engine orchestrating all readers.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is the primary interface for Modules 2 and 3. It provides:
1. Context manager for easy measurement (\sphinxtitleref{with engine as m:})
2. High‑frequency sampling (Req 1.46)
3. Idle baseline measurement (Req 1.45) \textendash{} stored separately, NOT applied
4. Core pinning (Req 1.15)
5. Measurement validation
6. Multiple‑run statistics

\sphinxAtStartPar
All readers are initialized from the configuration passed in.

1. Captures snapshot values (RAPL, scheduler)
2. Starts continuous readers (perf, turbostat)
3. Starts high\sphinxhyphen{}frequency sampling

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
gui/components/section\_landing.py
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Generic section landing page — renders the card grid for any section.
Called by streamlit\_app.py when nav\_page is None.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Section hero: accent colour, icon, title, who, description

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Resume chip: “↩ Resume: Energy Lab” if last\_page exists for this section

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Card grid: 3 columns, each card shows icon, label, desc, status badge

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Blocked cards: show data gap reason instead of clickable card

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Planned cards: shown greyed out, not clickable

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Clicking a card sets nav\_page \(\rightarrow\) st.rerun()

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar

Design conditional multi\sphinxhyphen{}container experiment plans.

\sphinxlineitem{Layout — two columns:}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}
\sphinxAtStartPar

✅ Save plans to config/experiment\_plans/ + push to execute queue
✅ Gap detection panel with {[}+ Add to Queue{]} buttons

\paragraph{gui.pages.dq\_sufficiency module}

\sphinxAtStartPar
gui/pages/dq\_sufficiency.py  —  ◈  Sufficiency Advisor
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FLAGSHIP DATA QUALITY PAGE.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Tells researchers exactly how many more experiments they need to run
before their data is statistically sufficient for each cell:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
cell = hardware (hw\_id) × model × task × workflow\_type
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Threshold: MIN\_RUNS\_PER\_CELL = 30 (configurable)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{For each cell shows:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Current run count

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Runs needed to reach threshold

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Progress bar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Which combinations have ZERO data (biggest gaps)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Overall dataset readiness score

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

\sphinxAtStartPar
gui/pages/dq\_validity.py  —  \(\checkmark\)  Run Validity
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Shows researchers which runs are flagged as invalid and why.
Flags checked:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
experiment\_valid = 0

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
thermal\_throttle\_flag = 1

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
baseline\_id IS NULL (no idle baseline — energy readings unreliable)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
thermal\_during\_experiment = 1 (thermal event during run)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
background\_cpu\_percent \textgreater{} 20 (noisy environment)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

\paragraph{gui.pages.silicon\_journey module}
\label{\detokenize{gui.pages.silicon_journey:module-gui.pages.silicon_journey}}\label{\detokenize{gui.pages.silicon_journey:gui-pages-silicon-journey-module}}\label{\detokenize{gui.pages.silicon_journey::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!gui.pages.silicon\_journey@\spxentry{gui.pages.silicon\_journey}}\index{gui.pages.silicon\_journey@\spxentry{gui.pages.silicon\_journey}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
gui/pages/silicon\_journey.py  —  Silicon Journey
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Environment\sphinxhyphen{}aware LLM interface. Minimal. Human\sphinxhyphen{}scale energy insights.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{UX philosophy:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Conversation dominates the screen

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Select task from dropdown \(\rightarrow\) prompt fills (same pattern as research\_insights.py)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Type freely \(\rightarrow\) custom \textendash{}task

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Animated st.status() while running — no logs

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reveal: “While you were chatting, something interesting happened in silicon…”

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Human\sphinxhyphen{}scale: phone \%, LED bulb, WhatsApp messages, water drops, CO\(\sb{\text{2}}\)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
“See what happened in silicon” \(\rightarrow\) inline deep analysis + charts + PDF

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retry on failure

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Command (copied exactly from execute.py):}\begin{optionlist}{3cm}
\item [\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}task\sphinxhyphen{}id]  
\sphinxAtStartPar
\textless{}id\textgreater{}   for predefined tasks
\item [\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}task]  
\sphinxAtStartPar
\textless{}text\textgreater{} for custom prompts
\end{optionlist}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\textendash{}provider cloud/local \textendash{}repetitions 1 \textendash{}country US \textendash{}cool\sphinxhyphen{}down 5 \textendash{}save\sphinxhyphen{}db

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Harness runs BOTH linear + agentic in ONE call — no dual threads.
Run\_ids fetched by latest exp\_id after completion.
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\index{render() (in module gui.pages.silicon\_journey)@\spxentry{render()}\spxextra{in module gui.pages.silicon\_journey}}

Central configuration for A\sphinxhyphen{}LEMS.

\sphinxlineitem{3\sphinxhyphen{}layer navigation:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Layer 1 — Sidebar:         11 section buttons  (SECTIONS)

Layer 3 — Page:            actual page module

\sphinxlineitem{Session state keys (set by sidebar.py, read by streamlit\_app.py):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
nav\_section  — active section name | None  \(\rightarrow\) None means Overview
nav\_page     — active page\_id      | None  \(\rightarrow\) None means section landing
nav\_last     — dict{[}section \(\rightarrow\) last page\_id visited{]}  (resume chip)

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar

\subsection{gui.connection module}
\label{\detokenize{gui.connection:module-gui.connection}}\label{\detokenize{gui.connection:gui-connection-module}}\label{\detokenize{gui.connection::doc}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!gui.connection@\spxentry{gui.connection}}\index{gui.connection@\spxentry{gui.connection}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
gui/connection.py
Manages live connection from HF Space to the laptop server via Cloudflare tunnel.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{st.session\_state{[}“conn”{]}:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
url       : permanent Cloudflare tunnel URL
token     : shared access token
verified  : bool \sphinxhyphen{} reached /api/online successfully
harness   : bool \sphinxhyphen{} measurement harness loaded on laptop
mode      : “offline” | “online”
error     : last error message

\end{description}
